page: Amazon Web Services m8a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs using AMD EPYC 9R45 processors running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. No local NVMe SSD storage or hardware GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results place the m8a.48xlarge in the top tier (top 10%) for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as measured by stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI). Memory bandwidth benchmarks show top-tier performance, reaching 1032.49 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 10%.

For large language model (LLM) workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models. However, text generation speeds show a relative tradeoff, ranking as strong (top 25%) for 7B and 70B models, and average (middle 50%) for the small 135M model. This indicates that while the CPU is highly efficient at initial prompt ingestion, token generation throughput is bound by CPU-only execution limits compared to GPU-accelerated alternatives.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8a.48xlarge offers high resource density and top-tier compute capabilities within a standard general-purpose hardware class, avoiding the premium of specialized GPU or high-memory instances. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale enterprise applications, high-throughput web serving, database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9R45 processors running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the m8a.48xlarge in the top tier (top 10%) for multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations. While prompt processing for LLMs is top-tier, text generation speeds represent a relative tradeoff, performing in the strong to average ranges. This instance is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, enterprise database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 192 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier compute and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.48xlarge features 192 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network speed for top-tier database and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density, 192-core AMD EPYC platform delivering top-tier compute performance and DDR5 memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Optimized for enterprise databases, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
